title: Dredging Corporation of India (DCI) Recruitment 2026, Notification Out for Fleet Personnel & Trainees, Walk-In Interview on 23rd June
shortInfo: Dredging Corporation of India Limited has released the DCI Recruitment 2026 Notification (Advt No. 02/2026) on its official website @dredge-india.com. A total of 45 trainee vacancies (15 TME + 15 TELO + 15 NCV(T)) have been announced along with openings for experienced Fleet Personnel on contract basis. Walk-in interviews begin on 23rd June 2026 at Dredge House, Visakhapatnam. The last date to send soft copy of bio-data is 17th June 2026 by 15:00 hours. Candidates for senior fleet ranks (Dredge Master, Chief Officer, Chief Engineer Officer, 2nd Engineer Officer) may also attend via VC Mode if

basic_details.job_title: Dredging Corporation of India (DCI) Recruitment 2026, Notification Out for Fleet Personnel & Trainees, Walk-In Interview on 23rd June
basic_details.short_information: Dredging Corporation of India Limited has released the DCI Recruitment 2026 Notification (Advt No. 02/2026) on its official website @dredge-india.com. A total of 45 trainee vacancies (15 TME + 15 TELO + 15 NCV(T)) have been announced along with openings for experienced Fleet Personnel on contract basis. Walk-in interviews begin on 23rd June 2026 at Dredge House, Visakhapatnam. The last date to send soft copy of bio-data is 17th June 2026 by 15:00 hours. Candidates for senior fleet ranks (Dredge Master, Chief Officer, Chief Engineer Officer, 2nd Engineer Officer) may also attend via VC Mode if

qualification.details: Candidates must have completed a 4-year Degree in Marine Engineering from any IMU-affiliated college / DG Shipping approved institute / State or Central Government recognised institute / AICTE-approved institute. Candidates holding a Graduate Marine Engineering certificate after completing a 4-year Engineering Degree are also eligible. Candidates who already hold or are about to obtain MEO Class IV COC are not eligible for TME. | Candidates must hold a 4-year Degree in one of the following disciplines from a State/Central Government or AICTE recognised institute: | Electrical Engineering Electronics Engineering Electrical & Electronics Engineering Electronics & Telecommunication / Communication Engineering Electronics & Instrumentation Engineering | Additionally, candidates must have completed a 4-month Electro Technical Officer (ETO) course from a DG Shipping approved institute and possess basic STCW 2010 certificates. | Candidates must have passed SSC / 10th Class from a recognised education board AND completed a Pre-Sea GP Rating course, passing the Exit Examination approved by DG Shipping with a minimum 60% aggregate marks. Age must be less than 25 years as on the date of joining on board. | All candidates must possess the following valid documents: | Indian CDC (Continuous Discharge Certificate) Indian Passport INDOS Number Basic STCW 2010 Certificates

vacancy_details.Required COC / COP / Experience: COC/COS as Master (FG) / Dredge Master Gr-I / Master (NCV)
Inland Master 1st Class / Inland Master 2nd Class
COC as 1st Mate (FG) / Dredge Master Gr-II / Mate (NCV)
COC as 2nd Mate (FG) / NWKO-NCV / Dredge Mate Gr-I with independent watch keeping experience
NWKO (NCV) certificate holder
MEO Class-I / Dredge Engineer Gr-I / MEO Class-II (12 months exp.) / MEO Class-III NCV-CEO with OSV endorsement (6 months exp.)
MEO Class-II / Dredge Engineer Gr-II / MEO Class-IV (FG) with 12 months exp. / MEO Class-III NCV-SEO with 12 months exp.
MEO Class-IV holder
Ex-CPO / PO / Leading Seaman (Survey/Navigation branch of Indian Navy) / NWKO(NCV) / Dredge Mate Gr-II / GPR with min. 2 years tube operator experience
COC as Rating (Engine Room Watch Keeping) with 3 months onboard experience as ERR-I
COC as Rating forming part of Engine Room Watch Keeping
COC as Rating (Navigational Watch Keeping) with 6 months onboard experience
COC as Rating forming part of Navigational Watch Keeping
Cook CDC (Cookery) / COC as Cook / Pea Sea Training in Catering Discipline

salary_details.details: DCIL provides stipend to all trainees during the training period. | Bank Guarantee Requirement: | TME / TELO: Rs. 3,00,000/- (must serve DCI for 5 years post-training) Trainee SHM / ERR / Cook: Rs. 1,00,000/- (must serve DCI for 2 years post-training)

selection_process: Fleet Personnel candidates will be selected based solely on their Interview performance. The minimum qualifying score is 50 marks out of 100.
Selection is based on a combined score of 100 marks:
80% weightage - Marks obtained in prescribed qualification 20% weightage - Interview performance Minimum cut-off: 50% in each component separately (i.e.
the percentage is calculated based on the 4-year Engineering Degree marks.
Selection is based on marks (no interview):
50% weightage - SSC / 10th Class marks 50% weightage - GPR Exit Examination marks
how_to_apply: Candidates can apply for DCI Recruitment 2026 through Walk-In Interview. Follow the steps below:
Visit the official DCI website at https://www.dredge-india.com/career.
Download the prescribed Bio-Data format available on the career page.
Fill in the Bio-Data form completely - include post applied for, date of birth, qualifications, CDC/Passport/INDOS details, and full experience history.
Attach self-attested photocopies of all required certificates (COC, CDC, Passport, INDOS, STCW 2010, Mark Sheets, Caste Certificate as applicable).
Send the soft copy of your Bio-Data and all documents to [email protected] on or before 17th June 2026 by 15:00 hours.

Senior fleet ranks unable to attend in person (Dredge Master, Chief Officer, Chief Engineer Officer, 2nd Engineer Officer) - mention "Request for attending Interview through VC Mode" in the subject line of your email.
Appear at the walk-in interview venue with original documents and one set of self-attested photocopies on the scheduled date.
Candidates currently on board (internal floating staff) who cannot sign off must attend via VC mode with the recommendation of the Master concerned.
Note: Candidates attending the Walk-In Interview under this advertisement need not apply online.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for DCI Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to send soft copy of Bio-Data and documents to [email protected] is 17th June 2026 by 15:00 hours. Applications received after this deadline will not be considered.

faq.question: Q2. When is the DCI Recruitment 2026 Walk-In Interview?
faq.answer: The walk-in interviews are scheduled on 23rd June 2026 (Fleet Personnel & Trainees ERR/SHM/Cook), 24th June 2026 (TME & TELO), and 25th June 2026 (NCV(T)) — all from 10:00 AM at Dredge House, Visakhapatnam.

faq.question: Q3. How many vacancies are there in DCI Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 45 confirmed trainee vacancies — 15 each for TME, TELO, and NCV(T). Fleet Personnel vacancies are based on operational requirements and are not fixed.

faq.question: Q4. What is the stipend for Trainee Marine Engineer in DCI 2026?
faq.answer: Trainee Marine Engineers will receive a stipend of Rs. 25,000/- per month plus Rs. 600/- victualing allowance per day during the training period.

faq.question: Q5. Can I attend the DCI interview via video call (VC Mode)?
faq.answer: Yes. Senior fleet ranks — Dredge Master, Inland Master, Chief Officer, 2nd Officer, Chief Engineer Officer, and 2nd Engineer Officer — who are unable to attend in person can request a VC Mode interview by mentioning it in the subject line of their email to [email protected] before 17th June 2026.

faq.question: Q6. What is the age limit for NCV Trainee in DCI Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates applying for NCV Trainee (NCV(T)) must be less than 25 years of age as on the date of joining onboard.

faq.question: Q7. Is DCI Recruitment 2026 a permanent job?
faq.answer: No. All vacancies under DCI Recruitment 2026 both fleet personnel and trainees are of a purely temporary contractual nature and do not confer any right to permanent or regular employment.

faq.question: Q8. What is the minimum qualifying score for Fleet Personnel selection?
faq.answer: The minimum qualifying score for Fleet Personnel selection through interview is 50 marks out of 100.

faq.question: Q9. Do I need to apply online for DCI Walk-In Interview 2026?
faq.answer: No. Candidates attending the walk-in interview under Advertisement No. 02/2026 need not apply online. Simply carry the prescribed Bio-Data form along with original and self-attested copies of all documents.

faq.question: Q10. Where is the DCI Walk-In Interview 2026 venue?
faq.answer: The interview will be held at DCIL, Dredge House, H.B. Colony, Seethammadhara, Visakhapatnam – 530022, Andhra Pradesh.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for DCI Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to send soft copy of Bio-Data and documents to recruitfs@dcil.co.in is 17th June 2026 by 15:00 hours. Applications received after this deadline will not be considered.

faq.question: When is the DCI Recruitment 2026 Walk-In Interview?
faq.answer: The walk-in interviews are scheduled on 23rd June 2026 (Fleet Personnel & Trainees ERR/SHM/Cook), 24th June 2026 (TME & TELO), and 25th June 2026 (NCV(T)) — all from 10:00 AM at Dredge House, Visakhapatnam.

faq.question: How many vacancies are there in DCI Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 45 confirmed trainee vacancies — 15 each for TME, TELO, and NCV(T). Fleet Personnel vacancies are based on operational requirements and are not fixed.

faq.question: What is the stipend for Trainee Marine Engineer in DCI 2026?
faq.answer: Trainee Marine Engineers will receive a stipend of Rs. 25,000/- per month plus Rs. 600/- victualing allowance per day during the training period.

faq.question: Can I attend the DCI interview via video call (VC Mode)?
faq.answer: Yes. Senior fleet ranks — Dredge Master, Inland Master, Chief Officer, 2nd Officer, Chief Engineer Officer, and 2nd Engineer Officer — who are unable to attend in person can request a VC Mode interview by mentioning it in the subject line of their email to recruitfs@dcil.co.in before 17th June 2026.

faq.question: What is the age limit for NCV Trainee in DCI Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates applying for NCV Trainee (NCV(T)) must be less than 25 years of age as on the date of joining onboard.

faq.question: Is DCI Recruitment 2026 a permanent job?
faq.answer: No. All vacancies under DCI Recruitment 2026 both fleet personnel and trainees are of a purely temporary contractual nature and do not confer any right to permanent or regular employment.

faq.question: What is the minimum qualifying score for Fleet Personnel selection?
faq.answer: The minimum qualifying score for Fleet Personnel selection through interview is 50 marks out of 100.

faq.question: Do I need to apply online for DCI Walk-In Interview 2026?
faq.answer: No. Candidates attending the walk-in interview under Advertisement No. 02/2026 need not apply online. Simply carry the prescribed Bio-Data form along with original and self-attested copies of all documents.

faq.question: Where is the DCI Walk-In Interview 2026 venue?
faq.answer: The interview will be held at DCIL, Dredge House, H.B. Colony, Seethammadhara, Visakhapatnam – 530022, Andhra Pradesh.
